checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the output and compared it to the source. There are no instances of text blocks appearing more times in the output than in the source. Each section corresponds to a specific page or group of pages without repetition of the text within or across sections.

The heading hierarchy within the converted sections (Pages 11-15) is consistent. The section titles act as primary organizational markers (mapping to H2 level), and subsections utilize H3 headings correctly. While pages 1-10 are missing, the hierarchy of the content that was successfully converted remains intact. The lack of an explicit H1 in the body is acceptable as the metadata contains a relevant title.

The source document contains a table on page 3. However, this page was not converted by the pipeline. Since no tables are present in the structured output, there are no structural corruptions to report.

The reading order of the output is significantly different from the source because the first 10 pages of the 15-page document are entirely omitted. The output begins with content from page 11 ('Storm Apps'). A final section ('Section 6') contains a placeholder message confirming that pages 1-11 were not successfully processed. This omission of more than two-thirds of the document at the start materially diverges from the source's intended structural flow and reading order.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source document begins on page 1 with 'NCDOT & NCDIT – T' and includes a timeline on page 4.
checks.b_evidence_output: The document starts with content from page 11 ('Storm Apps') and includes a final paragraph stating: 'Content from pages 1-11 could not be converted.'

checks.a_analysis: The lists on pages 11, 12, and 13 were correctly identified and formatted. Nesting is correctly represented (e.g., the sub-bullet 'Majority are GIS...' on page 12 is nested under the parent item), and list items match the source text accurately.
The primary government URL 'it.nc.gov' on page 15 is correctly identified and linked in the output. No broken or fabricated links were found in the converted sections of the document.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output is missing approximately the first two-thirds of the document content. The source PDF consists of 15 pages, but the HTML rendering begins with the content from Page 11 ('Storm Apps'). Pages 1 through 10, which contain the title page, introduction, road closure data, and the event timeline, are entirely absent from the rendered body. While there is a metadata note stating '[Pages 1-11 unavailable]', the visual organization of the document is fundamentally compromised as a user cannot access the majority of the original presentation's information.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains 15 pages of content, starting with a title slide 'NCDOT & NCDIT – T Florence Response Utilizing GIS'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML output begins with the 'Storm Apps' section (from PDF page 11) and includes a footer message stating '[Pages 1-11 unavailable]'. Content from pages 1-10 is missing.
checks.b_evidence_location: Entire document layout, specifically the start of the body content.

checks.a_analysis: The only table in the source document is located on Page 3 ('Road Type' table). Because Page 3 was not rendered in the HTML output, there are no tables present to evaluate for visual rendering quality. Since no tables are visually broken or corrupted in the output (as none exist), this check technically passes regarding rendering quality, with the omission handled under V1.

The majority of meaningful visual content from the source PDF is missing in the HTML output. This includes the line graph on page 3, the complex timeline graphic on page 4, map screenshots on pages 5 and 9, flooding photos on pages 6 and 7, and the orbital hurricane image on page 13. Additionally, several social media icons (Facebook, LinkedIn, Flickr) from the final 'Let's Connect!' page (page 15) are missing, with only the Twitter and YouTube icons rendered at disproportionately large sizes. While the satellite image from page 12 is present, the absence of the other critical data visualizations and photos is a major failure.
checks.b_evidence_source: Meaningful visual elements throughout the PDF: Line graph (p. 3), Timeline chart (p. 4), Maps (p. 5, 9), Photos (p. 6, 7), Orbital hurricane photo (p. 13), and social media icons for Facebook, LinkedIn, and Flickr (p. 15).
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML output lacks these visuals. For example, the orbital hurricane photo from PDF page 13 is missing before the 'Questions...' heading, and the final section lacks three of the five social media icons from the source.
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the document, specifically pages 3-10, 13, and 15.

checks.a_analysis: There is no evidence of body content blocks, such as paragraphs or lists, being duplicated in the HTML output. The repetition of section titles in the Table of Contents and as body headings is a standard accessibility feature and does not constitute a defect.
